name: Will Vodery
abstract: Will Vodery (October 8, 1885 – November 18, 1951) was an American composer, conductor, orchestrator, and arranger, and one of the few black Americans of his time to make a name for himself as a composer on Broadway, working largely for Florenz Ziegfeld. He had offices at the Gaiety Theatre office building in Times Square.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Whaley
about_quotes.evidence: I met Bill Vodery here in New York, when I went in the Army in 1918, he was bandmaster.

about_quotes.speaker: Jacques Butler
about_quotes.evidence: The mention of Will Vodery appears only in a [NOTE] by the interviewer ('them, with Will Vodery,') and is not confirmed or elaborated upon by the interviewee in the provided text.
